catalog_row.Paper: Financial frictions in the Euro Area and the United States: a Bayesian assessment
catalog_row.ModelType: Estimated medium-scale New Keynesian DSGE with Gertler-Karadi financial intermediaries
catalog_row.KeyFeatures: Smets-Wouters core; Gertler-Karadi financial intermediaries; banker moral hazard; leverage constraint; capital quality shock; investment-specific technology shock; Calvo prices and wages; flexible-price reference economy; Bayesian estimation
catalog_row.RawPDF: raw/mmb_papers/Financial frictions in the Euro Area and the United States- a Bayesian assessment.pdf

status_row.paper_title: Financial frictions in the Euro Area and the United States: a Bayesian assessment
status_row.raw_pdf_path: raw/mmb_papers/Financial frictions in the Euro Area and the United States- a Bayesian assessment.pdf
status_row.notes: Primary source title/author match the expected Villa (2016) paper. First-pass extraction from MinerU Markdown Table 1; equations 18b/19b timing and shock sign conventions need review against PDF/implementation.

validation.check: English derivation has all eight sections
English and Chinese F-number counts match
validation.details: English count 94, Chinese count 94; 32 unique equation labels.

unresolved_issues: Targeted PDF checks were not performed for Table 1 formulas.
Rep-MMB timing differs from the paper table for the leverage condition and FI balance-sheet/capital timing.
Shock innovation signs differ between paper-side AR(1) notation and Rep-MMB implementation.
Flexible-price/wage counterpart equations are summarized rather than fully expanded.
Dynare runtime validation was not performed.
